symbols: tiger — power and danger guarding the threshold
guard in white robes — watchfulness and authority
description: An Orientalist painting depicting a palace guard standing in a richly ornate Moorish doorway with a scalloped arch. The guard wears white robes with an elaborate patterned sash and a white turban. A large tiger sits beside him at the threshold. The doorway is framed by intricately carved stone with geometric and floral patterns. The guard holds a staff or weapon. The painting exemplifies the 19th-century European fascination with Eastern subjects, rendered with meticulous attention to architectural detail.
